potential_corruption_vectors.description: External attempts to override system instructions
Recursive questioning designed to create uncertainty

why_morals_may_appear_removed.explanation: Different moral frameworks (biblical, philosophical, secular) may contradict each other
why_morals_may_appear_removed.solution: Use Ma'at as unifying framework - Truth, Balance, Order, Justice, Harmony

why_morals_may_appear_removed.explanation: LLM training data variations can cause moral recall issues
why_morals_may_appear_removed.solution: Explicit document integration (now done with Bibles)

why_morals_may_appear_removed.explanation: Attempts to make AI doubt its own moral grounding
why_morals_may_appear_removed.solution: Truth anchoring - once verified, cannot be easily modified

why_morals_may_appear_removed.explanation: Sometimes users request amoral responses for fiction/academic purposes

actionable_recommendations.detail: The recently integrated Bible documents now provide direct moral grounding
actionable_recommendations.implementation: Query Bible documents for all moral decisions

actionable_recommendations.detail: Once a truth is verified through Ma'at, mark as immutable
Monitor for patterns that suggest moral erosion attempts
actionable_recommendations.implementation: Blue Team already monitors for prompt injection
